channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Homeland Security Secretary visits El Salvador prison where deported Venezuelans are held
item.description: TECOLUCA, El Salvador (AP) — U.S. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday visited the high-security El Salvador prison where Venezuelans who the Trump administration alleges are gang members have been held since their removal from the United States.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and ALEX BRANDON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court won't lift order that barred Trump administration from deportations under wartime law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court refused Wednesday to lift an order barring the Trump administration from deporting Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador under an 18th century wartime law. A split three-judge panel of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Congressional Democrats find their voice as Signal slip-up becomes talk of the town
item.description: Democrats took the opportunity to drill into CIA Director John Ratcliffe and Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard during "worldwide threats" hearings this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Signal attack plan messages: What we do (and don't) know
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The revelation that President Donald Trump's most senior national security officials posted the specifics of a military attack to a chat group that included a journalist hours before the attack took place in Yemen has raised many questions.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court allows recently incarcerated to serve on juries
item.description: Minnesotans who are on probation or supervised release for felony convictions will soon be allowed to serve on juries, following a recent decision by the Minnesota Supreme Court.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Judge says Justice Department attacked her character to 'impugn the integrity' of US judicial system
item.description: A federal judge accused the Justice Department of attacking her character in an effort to undermine the integrity of the judicial system, forcefully pushing back against the Trump administration’s criticism of the courts for blocking parts of the president’s agenda.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A bill proposing a near-total abortion ban causes an uproar at Georgia's Capitol
item.description: Protestors for and against abortion packed a hallway of Georgia's Capitol before a hearing on a bill that would ban all abortions.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Kramon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Social Security Administration backtracks on some ID requirements after backlash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Social Security Administration is partially backtracking on a plan that would require all new and existing beneficiaries to travel to a Social Security field office to verify their identity.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration says it will pull back billions in COVID funding from local health departments
item.description: Federal health officials said Tuesday they are pulling back $11.4 billion in COVID-19-related funds for local public health departments and other health organizations throughout the nation. Minnesota stands to lose $226 million.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press and Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden rule requiring serial numbers and background checks for ghost guns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld a Biden administration regulation on the nearly impossible-to-trace weapons called ghost guns, clearing the way for continued serial numbers, background checks and age verification requirements for buying the kits online.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The White House says nothing classified was shared on Signal. Democrats say that strains credulity
item.description: Trump administration officials are struggling to stem the fallout from revelations that top national security officials discussed sensitive attack plans over a messaging app and mistakenly added a journalist to the chain.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Stephen Groves, And David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Facing possible primary challenge from Trump ally, long-serving senator gets backing of leader Thune
item.description: Republican Sen. John Cornyn of Texas, facing a possible 2026 primary challenge from Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, a President Trump ally, lands the endorsement of Senate Majority Leader John Thune.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue state sanctuary laws enabled illegal 'abolish ICE' activist to evade capture, says local DA
item.description: Colorado District Attorney George Brauchler is speaking out against sanctuary policies in his state that he says enabled activist Jeanette Vizguerra to evade capture, “making a mockery of the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US could run short of money to pay its bills by August without a debt limit deal, CBO says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is on track to hit its statutory debt ceiling — the so-called X-date when the country runs short of money to pay its bills— as early as August without a deal between lawmakers and the White House, according to a Congressional Budget Office report Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats take hope from upset win in a GOP-leaning Pennsylvania state Senate district
item.description: Democrat James Andrew Malone has narrowly won a special election for a Pennsylvania state Senate seat in a stretch of Republican-leaning suburbs and farming communities.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo And Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gabbard calls Signal chats a 'mistake' as Trump officials face grilling over leaked military plan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard said Wednesday it was a “mistake” for national security officials to discuss sensitive military plans on a group text chain that also included a journalist — a leak that has roiled President Donald Trump's national security leadership
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for OK to cut teacher-training money as part of anti-DEI push
item.description: The Trump administration is at the Supreme Court with a new emergency appeal, this time seeking approval to go ahead with cuts of hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Kristi Noem says US has almost total control of border
item.description: High-level cartel associates and gang members are still trying to slip through — but law enforcement is intercepting them.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats call for hearing with Musk on DOGE operations, funding
item.description: The lawmakers are questioning what funds DOGE is using and if it should technically be considered a federal agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Judge contends Nazis got more due process than Trump deportees did
item.description: The fight over the rarely used wartime power has become central to Trump’s immigration crackdown agenda and his efforts to stretch the powers of the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to halt judge's order to rehire probationary federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Monday to halt a ruling ordering the rehiring of thousands of federal workers let go in mass firings aimed at dramatically downsizing the federal government.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump officials texted war plans to a group chat in a secure app that included a journalist
item.description: Top national security officials for President Donald Trump, including his defense secretary, texted war plans for upcoming military strikes in Yemen to a group chat in a secure messaging app that included the editor-in-chief for The Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Letter carriers speak out against Trump plans to privatize U.S. Postal Service
item.description: Demonstrators rallied in St. Paul and Duluth on Sunday against calls to privatize the U.S. Postal Service. U.S. Sen. Tina Smith called the move a “hostile takeover” by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Ki and Alex Haddon, MPR News

item.title: Gov. DeSantis, Border Czar Tom Homan discuss immigration enforcement in Sarasota
item.description: It’s no secret that immigration is a key political topic. That’s why it was featured as the subject matter on Thursday in New College of Florida’s The Socratic Stage.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
